title: ATP Execution Contract
description: Defines authority, policy, approval, credential access, and evidence requirements for governed agent execution under the Agent Trust Protocol. See ATP-SPEC-v1.md Section 4.

version.description: Semantic version of the contract format.

authority.description: Organization-scoped authority URI. Format: org.{domain}.{permission}. Validated against the organization's capability registry. See spec Section 5.1.

template.description: ATP template ID. References a reusable policy template registered on DUAL.

actions.description: Permitted action names. The agent may only invoke actions listed here.

scope.description: Action-specific constraints evaluated before execution. Supports enumeration, numeric bound, pattern, temporal, boolean, deny list, rate limit, and size limit constraint types. See spec Section 6.2.

approval.description: Approval configuration. See spec Section 7 for the full approval state machine.

required.description: Whether approval is mandatory for this action.

required_above.description: Monetary threshold above which approval becomes mandatory. Null means always required (if required is true).

approver_role.description: Organizational role authorized to approve this action.

timeout.description: ISO 8601 duration. How long before approval times out. Contracts with timeouts under 60s default to synchronous mode. See spec Section 7.5.

escalation_path.description: Comma-separated organizational roles for escalation if primary approver is unavailable. Each target receives a fresh timeout of the same duration.

credentials.description: Credential brokerage configuration. The gateway acts as a credential broker — agents never see credentials. See spec Section 8.

provider.description: Credential provider identifier (e.g., gmail-api, stripe-api, internal-db).

scope.description: Maximum credential scope the action may use. Gateway MUST NOT inject a credential with broader scope. See spec Section 8.3.

inject_as.description: How the credential is injected into the downstream request. See spec Section 8.4.

fail_closed.description: If true, credential injection failure causes action denial. MUST be true for production contracts. See spec Section 8.6.

output.description: Expected output configuration. Defines the governed DUAL object created or modified by this action. See spec Section 14.3.

object_type.description: DUAL object type for the output.

initial_state.description: Initial state of the output object after successful execution.

schema_ref.description: Reference to a JSON Schema defining the output object structure.

attestation.description: Evidence level. 'full' records complete execution context on DUAL. 'light' records action metadata only. 'none' skips attestation (development only). Production contracts MUST use 'full' or 'light'. See spec Section 10.2.

revocable.description: Whether this contract can be revoked. Revocation immediately invalidates all pending approvals and blocks new executions. See spec Section 11.5.

expiry.description: ISO 8601 datetime. Absolute contract expiration. Triggers automatic revocation. See spec Section 11.5.

idempotency.description: Idempotency model. 'gateway-enforced': ATP assigns HMAC-SHA256 idempotency keys and prevents duplicate execution. 'tool-native': downstream tool handles dedup. 'unsafe': retries may cause duplicate side effects (requires idempotency_ack in scope). See spec Section 11.1.

execution_timeout.description: ISO 8601 duration. Maximum time the gateway waits for downstream tool response. Default: PT30S. Gateway enforces a maximum ceiling. See spec Section 9.4.

delegation.description: Sub-delegation configuration. Controls whether the agent can delegate authority from this contract to other agents. See spec Section 5.2.

allow_sub_delegation.description: Whether the agent may re-delegate authority to another agent.

max_depth.description: Maximum delegation chain depth. 0 means no sub-delegation.
